description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated figure in a meditative pose, with a multi-headed serpent, likely a naga, fanned out protectively behind and above the figure. The figure sits cross-legged on a tiered, rounded base, and the serpent's heads are detailed with open mouths and expressive features. The back of the sculpture reveals the coiled body of the serpent and a stylized spiral motif at the center. The material appears to be weathered stone, giving the surface a rough, matte finish with visible textural details. The sculpture rests on a wooden or similarly colored support platform, which is not part of the main artwork. The overall form and iconography suggest influences from Southeast Asian or South Asian religious art, possibly representing the Buddha sheltered by Mucalinda.
description: A stone sculpture of a meditating figure sheltered by a multi-headed serpent, with detailed texturing and a rough, matte finish.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the seated figure, the multi-headed serpent fanned above, and the detailed facial and body features.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the serpent's heads and the layered, coiled base beneath the figure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the coiled serpent body and a spiral motif at the center, with no visible figure details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the figure and the serpent heads, emphasizing the sculpture's thickness.
attribute_subject_matter: seated meditative figure with multi-headed serpent (naga)
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a wooden platform, suggesting an indoor or protected exhibition setting, possibly a museum or gallery.
